questions.question: The correct group of animals that suffer from the “Foot & Mouth ” disease is as follows:
questions.explanation: Foot and mouth disease (FMD) or hoof and mouth disease (HMD) is an infectious and sometimes fatal viral disease that affects cloven-hoofed animals. Cattle, Sheeps and Pigs are affected by the Foot & Mouth disease.

questions.question: The Bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE) is commonly known as _______?
questions.explanation: The Bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE) is commonly known as Mad Cow Disease. It is a brain disorder in an adult cattle that may be spread to humans through diseased meat.

questions.question: White-nose syndrome (WNS) is an emerging disease among which of the following fauna?
questions.explanation: White-nose syndrome (WNS) is an emergent disease of hibernating bats that has spread from the north-eastern to the central United States at an alarming rate.

questions.question: Malaria can be caused by:
questions.explanation: The Plasmodium parasite that causes malaria is neither a virus nor a bacterium – it is a single-celled parasite that multiplies in red blood cells of humans as well as in the mosquito intestine. When the female mosquito feeds on an infected person, male and female forms of the parasite are ingested from human blood.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is spread by mosquitoes but is not a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Filariasis is a parasitic disease caused by an infection with roundworms of the Filarioidea type. These are spread by blood-feeding black flies and mosquitoes. Meningitis is an inflammation of the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord known as the meninges. The inflammation may be caused by infection with viruses, bacteria or other microorganisms. Yellow fever is an acute viral disease. It is caused by the yellow fever virus and is spread by the bite of an infected female mosquito.
